Featured Series

Pilgrim on God & Salvation
Pilgrim on the Path to Eternity

Our Story

About Puritan Publishing

The Puritans wrote for people in dungeons. Grief. Doubt. Depression. Dying. Every season of the soul. Their words still meet us there — and show us the way out.

Richard Sibbes wrote The Bruised Reed for souls so damaged they could barely lift their heads. Thomas Watson wrote for people in grief, in doubt, in the long silence of unanswered prayer. John Bunyan wrote Pilgrim's Progress from a Bedford jail cell.

They had answers that still work. The problem is that most people today can't read them.

Puritan Publishing exists to change that — for every kind of reader, in every season of life.

"Seasons change. Truth doesn't. Whatever season you find yourself in — the Puritans have been there before you."

Three Readers. Three Offerings.

We recognize that readers come to these books differently, and we honor each approach.

For the purist — For each title we publish, we provide a free link to the original public domain text, exactly as written, on our Resources page. No charge. No alteration. Just the source.

For the traditionalist — Our Original Seasons series preserves every word of the original language while making the text navigable: spelling corrected, punctuation clarified, long sentences divided, subheadings inserted, chapter summaries added, and eight study questions concluding each chapter. Available in print and ebook. Prepared by human editors. The language is the author's own.

For the modern reader — Our New Seasons series offers fully modernized editions for today's readers and listeners. The language is updated. The theology is untouched. Available in print, and many in audio.

How Puritan Publishing Began

I am Steve Larsen — audiobook narrator, lifelong reader of old books, and the unlikely founder of a publishing house.

A few years ago I was recording works by John Bunyan. The material was extraordinary — powerful, necessary, life-changing. I began sharing printed chapters with friends. One after another, they told me the same thing: "I don't understand it." "It's too hard." "I'll wait for your audiobook."

Those responses haunted me. The books that had shaped my faith were inaccessible to the people I loved most. Something had to be done.

But I was one person. I could modernize perhaps twenty titles in a lifetime. The scale of what needed doing was enormous. That conviction led me to Ryan Kaufman — a brilliant language scholar, a seminary graduate, a committed Christian, and, like me, deeply skeptical of Artificial Intelligence. I told him what I was trying to build. By God's grace, he said yes.

And then, by God's grace, Brad Sundt came into our lives. We had the idea. We had the product. Brad brought the vision for how to get it into the world. His partnership has been a gift we didn't see coming.

A word about our use of AI — read our full disclosure here.

Glory be to God,
Steve Larsen
Puritan Publishing LLC

The Team

The People Behind the Books

PHOTO COMING
Steve Larsen
Founder · Publisher · Narrator · Editor

Steve has been reading Puritan literature for decades. A professional audiobook narrator with over 30 years of immersion in the Reformed tradition, he founded Puritan Publishing to bring these books to the people who need them most.

PHOTO COMING
Ryan Kaufman
Director of Technology · Editor

A language scholar, seminary graduate, and committed Christian, Ryan built the editorial and AI pipeline that makes modernization possible without compromising the theology. His work is prayerful, precise, and distinctly human.

PHOTO COMING
Brad Sundt
Director of Marketing & Promotion · Editor

Brad came into the Puritan Publishing story by God's grace at exactly the right moment. He brings the vision and energy to get these books into the hands of the people who need them — and the wisdom to know how to reach them.

Transparency

A Note on Our Use of AI

— A personal word from Steve Larsen, Founder

I'll be direct: I spent years with serious misgivings about artificial intelligence. As a voice actor and audiobook narrator I have firsthand knowledge of the damage AI has caused in those industries. No abstraction here — my misgivings were based in real, tangible, negative experiences.

But the scale of what needed doing — potentially thousands of books, with who knows how many readers waiting. I wanted our efforts to be distinctly human. I also believed that distinctly human effort was going to require powerful tools. The question was not whether to use AI, but how — and our answer was temperance.

As I pondered, wrestled, and prayed about this, my friend Ryan Kaufman came to mind. Ryan is a mature Christian, a language scholar with an MDiv from Reformed Theological Seminary, and a technological savant whose misgivings about AI mirror my own — but are more informed, as he understands things from a technological viewpoint.

So I approached Ryan with the concept of Puritan Publishing, told him about my idea to modernize old Christian books currently in the public domain, gave him an idea of the scale, and asked him if he'd like to join me. By God's grace, he said yes.

Together we wrestled through the ethics of using AI as a tool in service of our human effort. By God's Providence, we are friends with, students of, and attend the same church as Dr. James Anderson — a Christian philosopher and ethicist who just so happens to hold a PhD in computer science. Dr. Anderson was gracious, listened carefully, and offered wise counsel that was both confirming and challenging.

Ryan then built something remarkable — a layered system that:

  • Preserves the author's original message, argument, and intent
  • Protects the theological content from any AI influence or alteration
  • Makes the tone understandable and relatable to today's reader
  • Replaces archaic language with modern equivalents

Every modernized text is then carefully edited and compared side-by-side with the original by human eyes — line by line, passage by passage — before publication.

If you are an AI skeptic — we honor that.

We only ask that if you're willing, you look at the side-by-side comparisons we provide with each modernized title. We believe you'll see our temperance and deliberate caution on full display.

Edited by humans. The theology is the author's own.

Coming Soon

New Voices

The Puritan tradition is not a museum. God is still raising up writers, teachers, and narrators who carry old light into new language.

🎧 New Audiobooks

Living Authors,
Narrated with Care

We are expanding our narration work to include new books by living Reformed and evangelical authors. Each title is selected for its pastoral depth and doctrinal faithfulness — the same standard we hold for our Puritan catalog.

Titles coming. Authors — inquire below.

📖 New Print & Ebook

Original Works,
Published with Integrity

Puritan Publishing is beginning to accept manuscripts from living authors whose work is biblically grounded, theologically sound, and written for the same readers we serve — people in every season of the soul.

Accepting inquiries. Submissions open soon.

✦ For Authors & Narrators

Do You Have
Something to Say?

If you are a writer with a manuscript or a narrator with a voice, we would love to hear from you. We are small, careful, and committed to quality over volume.
